questions.question: What is composition in object-oriented programming?
options: A class inheriting behavior from a parent class
A class containing objects of other classes as fields
A class implementing multiple interfaces
A class that cannot be instantiated
questions.answer: A class containing objects of other classes as fields

questions.question: Given the classes Address and Person, where Person has a field 'private Address homeAddress', what type of relationship does this represent?
options: An "is-a" relationship
A "has-a" relationship
A "uses-a" relationship
An inheritance relationship
questions.answer: A "has-a" relationship

questions.question: What does the @Override annotation indicate when placed above a toString() method?
options: The method is being defined for the first time
The method replaces a method inherited from a superclass
The method is private and cannot be called externally
The method will be called automatically at object creation
questions.answer: The method replaces a method inherited from a superclass

questions.question: If two Person objects share a reference to the same Address object, what happens when the Address is modified through one Person?
options: Only that Person's address changes
A copy of the Address is automatically created
Both Person objects see the change because they reference the same object
A NullPointerException is thrown
questions.answer: Both Person objects see the change because they reference the same object

questions.question:
Which line correctly accesses a nested composed object's method?

person.getAddress().getCity()
person.Address.getCity()
person.homeAddress.city
person->getAddress()->getCity()
options: person.getAddress().getCity()
person.Address.getCity()
person.homeAddress.city
person->getAddress()->getCity()
questions.answer: person.getAddress().getCity()

questions.question: A ContactInfo class requires that the email field must contain '@'. Where should this validation be placed?
options: In the main method only
In the getter method only
In both the constructor and the setter method
In the toString() method
questions.answer: In both the constructor and the setter method

questions.question: What is the purpose of a no-arg constructor that sets fields to placeholder values?
options: To prevent the class from being instantiated
To allow object creation when specific data is not yet available
To make all fields public
To override the parent class constructor
questions.answer: To allow object creation when specific data is not yet available

questions.question: In a Playlist class, 'private ArrayList<MusicTrack> tracks' is an example of:
options: Inheritance with a collection
Composition with a collection of objects
A static field
An abstract data type
questions.answer: Composition with a collection of objects

options: Method overriding
Constructor chaining using this()
Recursive constructor call
Static factory method
questions.answer: Constructor chaining using this()

questions.question: A MusicTrack class has a setRating(double rating) method that only accepts values between 0.0 and 5.0. What should happen if the value 6.5 is passed?
options: The rating should be set to 6.5 without any check
The method should silently ignore the call or set a default value
The field should be set to null
The program should always terminate immediately
questions.answer: The method should silently ignore the call or set a default value

questions.question: What is the purpose of a static field 'totalPlaylists' in a Playlist class?
options: To store the number of tracks in each playlist
To track how many Playlist objects have been created across all instances
To limit the number of tracks a playlist can hold
To store the name of the most recent playlist
questions.answer: To track how many Playlist objects have been created across all instances

questions.question: Which principle of good class design ensures that fields are not directly accessible from outside the class?
options: Polymorphism
Encapsulation using private fields with public getters and setters
Abstraction through interfaces
Inheritance through extends
questions.answer: Encapsulation using private fields with public getters and setters

questions.question: In a LibraryBook class, the checkOut() method decreases availableCopies. What validation should it perform?
options: Check that the book title is not empty
Check that availableCopies is greater than 0 before decrementing
Check that the ISBN is valid
Check that the author is not null
questions.answer: Check that availableCopies is greater than 0 before decrementing

questions.question: What does System.out.println(myObject) call on myObject if toString() has been overridden?
options: The hashCode() method
The equals() method
The overridden toString() method
The getClass() method
questions.answer: The overridden toString() method

questions.question: A LibraryMember has a field 'private int memberId' that is auto-assigned from a static counter. Which approach correctly implements this?
options: Setting memberId in the getter method
Incrementing a static counter in the constructor and assigning it to memberId
Using a random number generator in the setter
Declaring memberId as a static field
questions.answer: Incrementing a static counter in the constructor and assigning it to memberId

questions.question: What is the difference between composition and inheritance?
options: Composition uses 'extends', inheritance uses 'has-a'
Composition models 'has-a' relationships, inheritance models 'is-a' relationships
Composition requires abstract classes, inheritance does not
There is no difference; they are interchangeable
questions.answer: Composition models 'has-a' relationships, inheritance models 'is-a' relationships

questions.question: A method addTrack(MusicTrack track) should only add a track if it is not already in the playlist. What should be checked?
options: Whether the track's duration is positive
Whether a track with the same title and artist already exists in the list
Whether the playlist name matches the track's album
Whether the track's rating is above 3.0
questions.answer: Whether a track with the same title and artist already exists in the list

options: The Singleton pattern
Providing a convenience constructor with sensible default values
The Factory pattern
The Observer pattern
questions.answer: Providing a convenience constructor with sensible default values

questions.question: In the expression person.getAddress().setCity("Seattle"), how many objects are involved?
options: One — only the person object
Two — the person object and the Address object returned by getAddress()
Three — person, Address, and String
Zero — this is a static method call
questions.answer: Two — the person object and the Address object returned by getAddress()

questions.question: Which of the following best describes why a returnBook() method should validate that availableCopies does not exceed totalCopies?
options: To ensure the program runs faster
To maintain data integrity and prevent logically impossible states
To satisfy the Java compiler's requirements
To allow multiple returns of the same book
questions.answer: To maintain data integrity and prevent logically impossible states

questions.question: A Playlist class has a method getMostPlayed() that returns the MusicTrack with the highest playCount. If the playlist is empty, what should this method do?
options: Return a new MusicTrack with default values
Return null or handle the empty case gracefully
Throw an unchecked exception without any message
Return the last track added to the playlist
questions.answer: Return null or handle the empty case gracefully

questions.question: What is the benefit of a displayFullProfile() method in a Person class that calls toString() on its composed Address and ContactInfo objects?
options: It eliminates the need for getters entirely
It delegates formatting responsibility to each composed object, following good OOP design
It makes the fields of Address and ContactInfo public
It prevents the Person class from being instantiated
questions.answer: It delegates formatting responsibility to each composed object, following good OOP design

questions.question: Which keyword is used in a constructor to call another constructor in the same class?
options: super
this
new
self
questions.answer: this

questions.question: A Library class has methods searchByTitle(), searchByAuthor(), and searchByGenre() that each return an ArrayList. Why is ArrayList the appropriate return type?
options: Because a search could match zero, one, or multiple books
Because ArrayList is the only collection type in Java
Because the method must return exactly one book
Because arrays cannot store objects in Java
questions.answer: Because a search could match zero, one, or multiple books

questions.question: A LibraryMember has a field 'private int maxBooks = 5' and a checkOutBook() method. What should checkOutBook() do if the member already has 5 books checked out?
options: Allow the checkout and increase maxBooks
Refuse the checkout and inform the user that the limit has been reached
Remove the oldest book to make room
Set maxBooks to 0
questions.answer: Refuse the checkout and inform the user that the limit has been reached
